timeline.label: Q1 2026 revenue $236.8M (+21% YoY); cash rose to $247.6M
Disclosed Bolt equity-method investment and data-center co-location initiative
Phase 2 desalination facility completed for May 2026 commercial operation target
Continued buybacks and regular dividend growth; no FY2025 special dividend
Horizon Kinetics reduced stake; governance transition context for activist oversight
FY2024 special dividends $3.33/sh alongside regular dividends
Accelerated water infrastructure build-out; TPWR revenue trajectory above $300M run-rate
Corporate conversion from trust structure to Texas Pacific Land Corporation
1888 Declaration of Trust assigns Permian surface and royalty estate; GAAP still carries Assigned interests at zero

industry.structure: Permian Basin surface and royalty ownership is highly concentrated among a few land-focused entities (TPL, LandBridge, private holders). Pricing power comes from irreplaceable acreage positions and long-dated easement contracts rather than operating scale.
industry.share_shift: TPL continues to acquire marginal royalty interests at disclosed transaction comps while expanding operated water infrastructure; peers such as LandBridge monetize powered-land and digital-infrastructure options on adjacent acreage.
industry.trend: Produced-water handling, power, and data-center co-location are emerging monetization layers atop traditional royalty and easement cash flows; regulatory scrutiny on produced-water disposal remains an industry-wide constraint.
